title: Design System: PS5 Urban Air Quality Intelligence

title.purpose: Zone names, screen and card titles.

body.purpose: Advisory messages and chat; cap prose at 65-75ch.

data.purpose: The AQI number; tabular figures so digits don't jitter.

caption.purpose: Timestamps, meta rows, provenance lines. Muted Ink.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Card/chip on hover; pairs with a 1-2px translateY.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Floating surfaces only - voice bar, menus, dialogs.

breakpoints.name: app
breakpoints.purpose: Max width of the single-column citizen app shell.

components.name: Primary Icon Button (Send)
components.description: Teal circular action for send/confirm; white glyph.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-primary" aria-label="Send"><svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M5 12h14M13 6l6 6-6 6"/></svg></button>

components.name: Mic Button
components.description: Voice input; switches to an alert fill with a slow pulse while listening (the one place motion signals live state).
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-mic" aria-label="Speak"><svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><rect x="9" y="3" width="6" height="11" rx="3"/><path d="M5 11a7 7 0 0 0 14 0M12 18v3"/></svg></button>

components.name: Quick-reply Chip
components.description: Tappable suggestion / filter; outline at rest, teal fill on hover and when selected.

components.name: Advisory Card
components.description: Flat white card, full 1px hairline border, no resting shadow. Severity is carried by the AQI indicator inside it, never a colored side-stripe.
components.css: .ds-card{display:flex;gap:10px;align-items:center;background:#fff;border:1px solid #d6dedd;border-radius:12px;padding:12px;color:#0e1a1c;transition:box-shadow 180ms,transform 180ms}.ds-card:hover{box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(9,20,28,0.08);transform:translateY(-1px)}.ds-aqi{width:56px;height:56px;border-radius:999px;display:grid;place-items:center;text-align:center;line-height:1;background:#ff9933;color:#0e1a1c}.ds-aqi[data-band="very-poor"],.ds-aqi[data-band="severe"],.ds-aqi[data-band="poor"]{color:#0e1a1c}.ds-aqi b{font:700 1.25rem/1 system-ui;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}.ds-aqi small{font-size:8px;opacity:.85}.ds-card-meta b{font:700 1rem/1.25 system-ui}.ds-card-meta span{display:block;font:400 0.78rem/1.2 system-ui;color:#47585c}

components.name: Chat Bubble (incoming)
components.description: Assistant message; white panel, hairline, one squared corner.
components.html: In ITO Junction the forecast is AQI 287 (Poor). Best to avoid outdoor activity.

components.name: Chat Bubble (outgoing)
components.description: The citizen's message; teal-whisper tint replacing the neon green.
components.html: Can my child play outside this evening?

components.name: Chat Input
components.description: Pill input; hairline at rest, teal ring on focus, muted placeholder that clears 4.5:1.

narrative.overview: This system carries the composure of an official air-quality advisory: the kind of notice a citizen reads and acts on, and an inspector cites in a decision. It is calm, legible, and sourced. Authority comes from restraint and evidence, not decoration - the interface recedes so the number, the band, and the recommended action stand forward. Warmth lives in the language and the human framing, never in the chrome. The palette is a cool, quiet neutral with a single deep-teal voice; the only loud colors on screen are the CPCB AQI bands, because severity is the message. It is positioned between the failures it rejects: the machine-made generic AI-SaaS look, the boring corporate-government portal, the childish messaging-app aesthetic its first draft leaned on, and any flashy or gimmicky motion.
keyCharacteristics: Cool neutral surface, one deep-teal action voice, CPCB AQI bands as the only saturated color.
One sans family, fixed rem scale, tabular figures for AQI numbers.
Flat by default; depth is a response to state, never ambient decoration.
Every severity is stated three ways - color, band name, number - never color alone.
Evidence is visible: sources are cited inline, not hidden.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Teal is the only chrome accent and appears only on interactive or brand elements - actions, selection, focus, header. It never decorates; its restraint is what makes the AQI bands read as urgent.

rules.name: The Legible Band Rule
rules.body: An AQI band fill carries white text only on Very Poor and Severe. Good, Satisfactory, Moderate, and Poor are too light for white and carry Ink text (#0e1a1c). Severity always pairs with the band name and number, so a color-blind reader loses nothing.

rules.name: The One Family Rule
rules.body: No display face, no second sans, no mono. Emphasis comes from weight or size, never family.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. A shadow means something changed state or is floating. If a card has a resting shadow for depth, delete it.

dos: Do ground the page in cool neutrals - Surface #f2f5f5 with Panel-white cards and 1px Line borders.
Do carry AQI severity three ways: band color on the indicator, band name, and number. Ink text on Good/Satisfactory/Moderate fills.
Do keep Deep Civic Teal as the single action voice; accent = actions, selection, focus only.
Do cite every number to its authority inline (CPCB / SAFAR / WHO / GRAP).
Do use one system sans at a fixed rem scale with tabular figures for data.
Do keep motion to state feedback - 150-250ms, ease-out - with a prefers-reduced-motion fallback.
donts: Don't use warm cream/sand/beige body backgrounds (the retired #e9e2db family) - the generic AI-SaaS tell.
Don't use neon/candy greens (#25d366) or WhatsApp-style messaging chrome; this is a public-health service, not a chat toy.
Don't use emoji as load-bearing UI or icons; keep them to sparse persona/domain accents.
Don't use side-stripe borders (border-left/right greater than 1px as a colored accent) on cards or callouts. The AQI indicator carries severity.
Don't put white text on light AQI bands (Good, Satisfactory, Moderate); it fails contrast.
Don't ship the corporate-government look: navy-and-grey, stock photos, clip-art icons, unstyled tables.
Don't reach for gradient text, glassmorphism-by-default, hero-metric templates, identical icon-card grids, or tiny uppercase tracked eyebrows.
Don't add decorative or gimmicky motion; motion conveys state, nothing else.
